hub.title: Zakat Categories
hub.subtitle: Choose a category to view the fatwa-based explanation.

introduction.title: Introduction
introduction.paragraph1: Zakat is given by the believer in response to the command of Allah. The Quran clarifies that its purpose is not only to help those in need but also to benefit the giver by purifying the soul and freeing it from greed and miserliness.
introduction.verse: "Take from their wealth a charity by which you cleanse and purify them." (Quran 9:103)
introduction.paragraph2: Therefore, Zakat is one of the pillars of Islam, one of its greatest obligations, and a fundamental foundation upon which many other rulings of Islamic life are built.

fatwa.title: Fatwa Basis (Arabic Excerpt)
fatwa.description: Tap to read the original wording.

detailedSetup.title: Detailed Calculation Setup
detailedSetup.subtitle: Before category selection, review how this flow works and set your hawl tracking preference for this session.

detailed.title: How does the detailed calculation work?
detailed.body1: The detailed calculator allows you to calculate zakat on multiple types of wealth at the same time, such as savings from salary, business assets, livestock, or agricultural products.
detailed.body2: The app calculates each category separately, then combines only the wealth that is currently subject to zakat in the final total.
detailed.noteBody: Not every category will be included in the final amount if its zakat is not yet due.

debt.title: How do debts affect zakat?
debt.body1: Debts can affect the amount of wealth on which zakat is calculated.
debt.body2: In the detailed calculation, the app considers:
debt.bullet1: money owed to you that is likely to be collected
debt.noteBody: This adjustment is applied only to monetary zakatable assets such as savings or business assets. Some categories like livestock or harvested crops are not adjusted by debts.

hawl.title: What is Hawl (the zakat year)?
hawl.body1: For some types of wealth, zakat becomes due after a full lunar year has passed while the wealth remains above the nisab. This is called hawl.
hawl.body2: In this step, we will determine how you track your zakat year so the app can identify:
hawl.bullet2: or whether it will be due later.
hawl.noteBody: Some categories, such as agricultural harvests, do not require a hawl and are due at harvest.

form.question: How do you track your zakat year?

options.yearly: I have a yearly zakat date
options.nisab: I know when my wealth first reached nisab

validation.trackingModeRequired: Please choose how you track your zakat year.
validation.estimatedChoiceRequired: Please choose how to start your estimated hawl tracking.
validation.invalidDate: Please enter a valid date in YYYY-MM-DD format.

detailedCalculator.instructions: Tap a category below to add it to your zakat calculation.

summary.title: Your Calculation Summary
summary.ruleAgriOther: Rule: 2.5% (one quarter of a tenth) if net value reaches nisab
summary.ruleTradeSector: Rule: 2.5% (one quarter of a tenth) if net amount reaches nisab
summary.ruleIndustrial: Rule: 2.5% after deducting production costs if net amount reaches nisab
summary.debtDoubtfulExcludedNote: Doubtful receivables are excluded until they are collected.
summary.debtAdjustmentSeeSection: Debt figures are shown in the Debt Adjustment section below.
summary.debtScopeNote: This adjustment applies only to cash-based categories such as salary, trade, and business income.
summary.belowNisabAfterDebtBody: Your debts reduced the adjusted zakatable base below the nisab. No zakat is due for this pool at this time.
summary.debtNoEligiblePoolNote: No eligible due-now money pool is present, so debt adjustment does not create a payable amount by itself.

salary.title: Salaries & Services
salary.description: Employment income, consulting, healthcare, legal fees

produce.title: Grains & Fruits
produce.description: Agricultural harvest or produce held for trade

trade_sector.title: Trade & Business
trade_sector.description: Commercial goods, stocks, currencies, company profits

debt.title: Debt
debt.description: Money owed to you or money you owe others

hawl.useCustomCategoryDate: Use custom date for this category

salary.scope: Scope: salaries/wages and service-based earnings (healthcare, legal, consulting, telecom, media rights, and similar services).
salary.modeHint: Annual mode: checks yearly net savings against nisab. Monthly mode: checks each month's net against nisab.

livestock.offspringHawlNote: Offspring born during the year follow the same hawl as their mothers.

agriOther.scope: Includes cultivated products for market, forest products, fishing products, and traded animal-production activities.
agriOther.rule: Rule: treated as trade assets. Zakat is on market value, not weight. If net value reaches nisab, due is one quarter of a tenth (2.5%).

trade.operatingCostsPlaceholder: Due operating costs (wages, rent, taxes, etc.)
trade.scopeMain: Includes commercial exchange of goods, assets with financial value, and lawful wealth convertible into money.
trade.scopeSecondary: Also includes stocks (shares), currencies, and income/proceeds of commercial companies and similar business earnings.
trade.rule: Calculation: subtract due business expenses, then pay 2.5% (one quarter of a tenth) if the remaining amount reaches nisab.

industrial.scope: Includes food manufacturing and broader industrial activities such as construction materials, metals, chemicals, electronics, textiles, energy equipment, and furniture.
industrial.rule: Rule: treated as commercial/productive business assets. Deduct production costs, then pay 2.5% if net value reaches nisab.

debt.owedToYouBody: Collectible receivables are counted in this cycle. Doubtful receivables are deferred until collection.
debt.youOweBody: Deduct due debt first, then check if remaining wealth is still above nisab.
debt.whatCountsLink: Not sure what counts as debt?
debt.whatCountsExamples: Typical examples: unpaid supplier invoices, short-term personal loans, and bills due before your zakat date.

produce.dueAtEventNote: This category becomes due at harvest/event time.
produce.tradeRule: Treated as trade goods: zakat is 2.5% if market value reaches nisab.
produce.harvestRule: Crop-zakat nisab is 653 kg (5 awsuq). If reached: 10% natural watering, 5% paid irrigation. Add price/kg to include a cash estimate in combined total.
